decisions.reasoning: Iron Hands threatens a 2HKO with Drain Punch, and Incineroar cannot secure a KO. Switching to Flutter Mane provides an immunity to the Fighting-type STAB and forces the opponent to respect a potential Fairy-type attack.
I outspeed Iron Hands and Moonblast is a guaranteed OHKO, fulfilling the primary decision priority.
Moonblast is a guaranteed 2HKO and I outspeed Tornadus-Therian, allowing me to secure the KO before they can threaten me significantly.
I outspeed Tornadus-Therian and Moonblast is a guaranteed 2HKO, which secures the KO this turn or next while Tornadus cannot OHKO me back.
Tornadus-Therian is at 8% HP, meaning Moonblast is a guaranteed KO. Since I outspeed, I can secure the kill this turn without needing to switch.
Moonblast is a guaranteed OHKO on Urshifu-Rapid-Strike, and since I outspeed, I can secure the knockout before they can use Surging Strikes to finish off my low-HP Flutter Mane.
Flutter Mane is fainted; I must send out a replacement. Incineroar is the best pivot to handle Urshifu-Rapid Strike due to Intimidate reducing the damage of Surging Strikes.

Incineroar is threatened with a 2HKO by Surging Strikes and cannot effectively damage Urshifu-Rapid. Switching to Calyrex-Ice preserves Incineroar for Intimidate support later and brings in a stronger offensive presence.

Calyrex-Ice is significantly slower than Urshifu-Rapid and is currently at 62% HP, meaning it will be KO'd by Surging Strikes. Switching to Incineroar allows me to Intimidate the Urshifu and pivot safely, preserving Calyrex-Ice for a later opportunity.
Incineroar is at 13% HP and will be KO'd by any move from Urshifu-RS. Using Fake Out provides chip damage and burns the opponent's turn, potentially allowing a safer switch-in for the next Pokemon.

Incineroar is at 13% and will be KOed by Surging Strikes. Switching to Calyrex-Ice preserves Incineroar for a potential late-game Intimidate or Fake Out while bringing in a threat that can force Urshifu out.

Calyrex-Ice is at 24% HP and will be KO'd by Surging Strikes this turn. Switching to Incineroar is the only way to preserve Calyrex-Ice for a potential late-game revenge kill or chip damage, as Incineroar can take the hit better than Calyrex-Ice.
Incineroar is fainted (0% HP). Dragonite is the best defensive pivot to handle Urshifu-Rapid-Strike's Surging Strikes due to its bulk and potential to retaliate.
Calyrex-Ice is at 24% and will be KO'd by Surging Strikes this turn. Switching to Dragonite preserves the potential for a Tera or priority move later while avoiding a wasted turn.
